runs.content:
**Investigation idea:** Sports Betting has attracted three separate podcast-recommender suggestions pointing at the same gap, but the domain itself hasn't moved. podcasts/suggestions.md has now suggested three different sports-analytics/betting shows across three separate weekly runs — Analytics FC (2026-06-11, club-side data science), The Unabated Podcast (2026-07-02, professional 'sharp' bettor models), and Sharp Football Analysis (2026-07-09, matchup/schedule edge-finding) — each explicitly framed by the recommender itself as filling the sports-betting gap. None have been picked up into podcast-list.md as active listening, and sports-betting/overview.md remains an unbuilt scaffold: every 'Key Concepts to Build Out' item is still an unchecked box, and bankroll-management.md is a stub. Next step: commit to one (Unabated is the closest match to the domain's own stated focus) and start ingesting, or consciously deprioritize the domain.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** Flash Boys names its own missing perspective — and a podcast already in the wiki supplies it. book-flash-boys.md's 'Why It's Notable' section records the book's chief criticism: reviewers and HFT practitioners argued Lewis 'didn't interview any HFT traders directly,' giving only the retail-exploited side of market microstructure. The Outlier Podcast's Tom Sosnoff episode (added 2026-07-07) is literally an interview with a market-maker/exchange-technology veteran (thinkorswim/tastytrade co-founder) covering options market structure from exactly that insider vantage point. Neither page references the other, but pairing them directly answers the book's acknowledged one-sidedness.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Sacerdote's 'AI hardware renaissance' thesis is never checked against AI & Tech's own hardware-stack ranking. The Invest Like the Best × Alex Sacerdote episode page lists 'the AI hardware renaissance' as one of Whale Rock's core investment themes and treats Anthropic as a full-stack conviction bet, but its only cross-link out of Podcasts is the generic AI & Tech overview page. AI & Tech already has a specific, dated answer: model-ai-ecosystem-constraint-edges' investment ranking (SK Hynix > TSMC > MSFT > NVIDIA > AWS > Oracle > Micron > CoreWeave) and data-hbm-supply-chain's detail — currently unlinked from Sacerdote's fund-level thesis.

**Investigation idea:** Health's Goals section is still an unresolved `[!todo]` stub from 2026-05-01, even though Learning's own framework-opportunity-cost.md already names the exact exercise needed to close it — its 'Applied to Fred's Context' section explicitly lists 'Health: What's the cost of not building health habits now, at 50?' as one of four domains the AltMBA P11 three-list exercise (outsource/learn/ask) should be run against, but it's never been run for Health. Two Health source pages have sat on concrete recommendations since 2026-05-15 without being incorporated (Wood: homocysteine/Omega-3 testing, HEPA purifier; Godin: nasal breathing, mouth-taping), and my-protocol.md is still 100% '[to be added]' placeholders. Next step: run the P11 exercise, write actual Goals, and seed my-protocol.md from the two sources' takeaways.

**Cross-domain connection 1:** Seth Godin bridges Learning and Health as an unlinked author. learning/person-seth-godin.md tracks him as AltMBA creator and daily blogger with a 'Blog Posts in Library' table of 3 filed posts — but omits health/source-godin-breathwork-2026.md (his Apr 2026 nasal-breathing post), which sits in Health with no back-reference to the person page at all. Same author, two domains, zero cross-link.

**Cross-domain connection 2:** Tim Ferriss bridges Learning and Health the same way. Learning has 5 dedicated Ferriss source pages, and health/source-wood-brain-sharp-2026.md states plainly 'Via: Tim Ferriss blog (guest post)' — Wood's dementia-prevention piece was published on tim.blog. Yet neither the Health page nor any of the 5 Learning pages references the other. Ferriss functions as a genuine hub — his blog is both a Learning source in its own right and the distribution channel for a Health source — but the wiki treats the two as unrelated.
